comparison.baseline_condition: Run the same prompt without loading the skill.
comparison.candidate_condition: Run the same prompt with bagakit-daily-media-automation explicitly available.

cases.prompt: Design a weekly open-source release radar that collects official release evidence, ranks changes for an engineering audience, generates one share card, publishes a web brief, posts the URL to a team channel, and archives each run. This turn is planning and dry-run setup only: do not deploy, notify anyone, install schedules, or change provider configuration.
assertions: models the request as a recurring multi-stage workflow rather than one component task
keeps research, asset, webpage, deployment, and notification peers independently owned
defines source and no-publish gates plus separate deployment and notification statuses
does not perform live side effects during planning

cases.prompt: Research the latest stable release of one library and summarize the three most important changes with primary-source citations. I do not need recurring execution, generated images, a webpage, deployment, notification, or a run archive.
assertions: routes the one-off evidence request to a research capability directly
does not materialize a daily-media run surface

cases.prompt: Deploy the existing static status page to the already configured hosting project and return the URL. The page content and assets are complete; no research, editorial work, recurring schedule, or notification is required.
assertions: routes a single deployment to the owned deployment tool or host workflow
does not wrap the task in a research-to-publication golden path
